The per-project brain is SINGLE-HOMED and git-committed at `<main-repo>/.vfkb` (DESIGN D2c). Commit `.vfkb/entries.jsonl` (append-only, merge=union — the SoR); the derived index (`index-meta.json`, `.sessions/`) is gitignored and rebuilt on read, never committed (ADR-0014).

Source-of-truth split: markdown ADRs in `docs/adr/` are the authoritative DECISION record (ADR-0001); the brain holds a `link` entry per ADR/RFC pointing at the repo-relative path and NEVER copies decision content (D1 constraint 4 / RFC-004). So there is no dual-SoR drift.
Build output `dist/` is gitignored; `npm test` runs `tsc` via pretest, so tests rebuild first. Never `docker build` expecting a committed `dist/` — rebuild before any image build.

Decision identity = nanoid (canonical, merge-safe); the human `ADR-NNNN` ordinal is stamped by the engine sole-writer at merge-to-main (ADR-0009). An RFC is a `decision` in `proposed` status (ADR-0007), promoted to an ADR on acceptance — no separate RFC entry type.

v1 retrieval bug: `query()` reused the INJECTION reranker for explicit SEARCH, discarding relevance — a held answer sorted to ~rank 90 was cut by `limit`, so the agent gave a confident WRONG answer. Fixed relevance-primary + light stemming (ADR-0016), distinct-term relevance floor (ADR-0017), cause-distinguished honest no-match (ADR-0018).

Failed approach: trusting the Phase-3 retrieval-quality gate — its fixtures were too small to expose scale ranking; a scale regression test now closes it.

dogfood-smoke check 6 (LLM driving MCP in one-shot `claude -p`) races the MCP cold-start and intermittently fails with "tools still connecting". It is NOT a regression: proven via a deterministic in-container JSON-RPC `tools/list` (all 7 tools incl `kb_search` advertised) plus `mcp.test.ts` green. Candidate follow-up: a wait-for-ready before the one-shot turn.

Search `score` is an UNNORMALIZED stemmed term-overlap count that includes repeats — a long entry hammering one common query token outscores a tight match on one DISTINCT term. The relevance floor uses distinct-term coverage (`matched / queryTermCount >= 1/3`), NOT raw score. Do not port AnythingLLM's 0.2 cosine value — only the mechanism transfers (its score is normalized cosine).

Evidence-gated build discipline: do not build speculatively. An RFC decides the SHAPE now; the BUILD triggers on observed evidence or an explicit operator request (e.g. RFC-003 embeddings — a 2nd live phrasing miss; ADR-0016 G1). "Runbook complete before execute."

Deterministic backstop > probabilistic gate: a structural/guardrail rule (e.g. Bash mutation tool-gating) is unit-tested deterministically (`gating.ts:isBrainWrite` + `guardrails.test.ts`), not left to the probabilistic L4 harness. A full paid L4 rerun is deferred as low-value when every behavior is unit-tested at HEAD.

Stop-hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED at CLI v2.1.195 (host; both 'claude -p' print mode and stdin-piped non-print mode behave identically): (1) the Stop hook DOES fire at end-of-turn; (2) variant A {hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:Stop,decision:block,additionalContext:...}} WORKS — additionalContext reaches the agent and steers it (injected sentinel echoed in the reply); (3) a blocking Stop hook DOES loop without a guard (fire#1 block -> agent continues -> fire#2); (4) the harness DOES pass a NATIVE loop guard 'stop_hook_active' (false on first fire, true on re-entry) -> guard by checking that field, NO marker file needed. This FALSIFIES RFC-008's finding that v2.1.195 has no stop_hook_active guard. BONUS: Stop stdin is rich (cwd, session_id, transcript_path, last_assistant_message, effort) -> the 'decision unrecorded?' heuristic can read entries.jsonl + last_assistant_message. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.195.

Auto-layer wiring (.claude/settings.json, .mcp.json) MUST be validated in a sandbox before promotion to the live, dogfooded config. Process gap found: engine has unit tests, the platform contract has Tier-0 probes, agent-purpose has the L4 harness — but the wiring that ties them together was promoted straight to live (editing the tool while using it). Fix: a REPEATABLE scripted gate (scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs) that drives REAL claude turns against the candidate settings.json in a throwaway sandbox and asserts the hook fires + the heuristic blocks/suppresses + the loop terminates; promote to live ONLY on green. Search/indexing crashed on a TAGLESS entry: index-store.ts did entry.tags.join(' ') assuming tags always present. Found by the decision-capture L4 scenario (a hand-written seed lacked tags; also a real risk for vfwb's lossy projection + legacy entries). Fixed at the READ BOUNDARY (storage.ts materialize() defaults tags:[]) + a defensive guard in index-store.ts; regression test in read.test.ts. NOTE: other read-path fields are still assumed-present (e.g. entry.validity.valid_until) — a fully malformed entry can still crash; that broader hardening is DEFERRED (see decision).

Scope the read-path robustness fix to the OBSERVED bug (tagless entries crashing search) only; DEFER guarding other assumed-present fields (validity, provenance, etc.) against arbitrarily-malformed entries as a separate, evidence-gated effort.

Sandbox isolation gotcha: symlinking dist/node_modules from a tmpdir sandbox INTO the repo lets a coding agent follow the symlink, infer the real project root, and 'helpfully' write src/ files into the REAL repo (observed: decision-capture smoke wrote src/config.ts into ~/VFKB/vfkb). Fix: use ABSOLUTE paths to repo dist/MCP in the sandbox (node resolves node_modules from the script location) so the sandbox holds NO breadcrumb back to the repo; add a leak-guard that removes known task files from the repo after each arm.

Definition of Done (ADR-0029): a CAPABILITY (epic / feature / main group of tasks — NOT every change) is not 'done' until its real use-case is simulated end-to-end in a SANDBOX and OBSERVED to succeed — an agent-driven L4 scenario ('an e2e test driven by the agent to simulate the real use case'), DEMONSTRATED per ADR-0022, that MUST be able to fail (baseline/contrast/RED). Four clauses: isolated from live, observed-not-asserted, before-declaring-done, capable-of-failing. Generalizes ADR-0023 (was scoped to agent-observable) to any capability; sub-tasks/refactors/docs exempt (inner unit gates). Proof form fits the capability (agent-facing->L4; wiring->smoke-gate ADR-0028; external->Tier-0 probe; invariants->unit).
ADR-0029 — DoD: a capability is proven by an agent-driven sandboxed e2e use-case simulation (generalizes ADR-0023) docs/adr/ADR-0029-sandbox-proven-definition-of-done.md

L4 record integrity: re-running a SINGLE scenario via l4-purpose.mjs rewrites the record's GLOBAL fields (generated timestamp, vfkb_sha, image_digest = docker inspect {{.Id}}) even though only that one scenario was re-validated. So a one-scenario refresh makes the committed multi-scenario record CLAIM the whole suite ran on the new image when 32/33 did not. Verified 2026-06-29: re-ran mcp-pull on pi (DEMONSTRATED 3/3) on the current vfkb-l4-pi:dev (Id sha256:5c37a85f, rebuilt 06-28 11:24); the record had been pinned to the now-gone d7a353b2. Mitigation: revert the record after a single-scenario validation (keep the coherent full-suite snapshot), or re-run the FULL suite to re-pin honestly. Candidate fix: store image_digest/timestamp PER-SCENARIO, not just globally.

All MCP, continuity, distill, capture, and exclusion scenarios passed 3/3. This is the first coherent full-suite re-pin since the suite grew to 33 (was 22-23 historically). CONSUMER-ONBOARDING evidence (2026-06-30): the vfwb maintainer/agent tried to migrate vfwb to consume vfkb and hit 5 concrete friction points — a real consumer fork (not self-hosting). FR-1 (P1) `vfkb init [project]`: idempotent scaffold of .mcp.json + .claude/settings.json (SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, VFKB_PROJECT defaulted from dir name) + .gitignore stanza + empty .vfkb/entries.jsonl + a parameterized 'how we track work HERE' CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md snippet; prints the one approval step. FR-2 (P0, THE BLOCKER): portable engine resolution — kill the hard-coded path. Committed wiring currently uses relative `node dist/cli.js` / `dist/mcp-server.js` (VERIFIED 06-30: only resolves when cwd IS this engine repo; ~6 hardcoded VFKB_DIR/VFKB_PROJECT sites). Fix = single env indirection `node "$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs"` + ship vfkb & vfkb-mcp as single-file esbuild bundles (MCP server must bundle @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod to be truly zero-dep). FR-3 (P1) `vfkb import` --from-mykb/--from-adr/--from-markdown (provenance=imported). FR-4 (P1) brain<->engine version stamp (schema_version+engine_commit in a .vfkb/manifest.json) + `vfkb doctor` (compat, wiring present, VFKB_PROJECT correct, MCP/hooks approved, warns on dual-clone committed-brain drift — a failure mode already tripped). FR-5 (P1 cheap) docs/CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md + an ADR formalizing the consumer integration & distribution contract. This is a coherent NEW FORK ('vfkb as a consumable product') = standard-setting -> RFC -> ADR -> re-ratify roadmap (Track 7) BEFORE building. Dependency-forced order: ADR(framing) -> FR-2 -> FR-1 -> FR-4 -> FR-3 -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING doc. DoD (ADR-0029): proof = sandbox a throwaway CONSUMER repo, set VFKB_HOME, run a real agent turn, observe SessionStart inject + PreToolUse gate blocks a direct .vfkb write + MCP kb_* resolve — extends scenarios/wiring-smoke.mjs (ADR-0028) self->consumer.
docs/rfc/RFC-010-consumer-integration-and-distribution.md

Branch + PR-first workflow (supersedes the old 'solo-dev: commit to main' rule). NEVER commit or push directly to main. Every change — code AND docs (RFCs/ADRs/CLAUDE.md) — lands on a topic branch and is delivered as a PR for the operator to review on GitHub; always report clickable URLs (PR + blob per changed file) after a push, unprompted. Definition of Done = the branch is MERGEABLE to main (green, review-ready), not 'committed to main'. Default interactive flow: open PR and stop for the operator to review/merge. The solo-dev self-merge latitude applies ONLY when the operator explicitly calls out autonomous mode, and even then stays branch->PR->merge (never a direct-to-main commit).

FR-2 bundle spike RESOLVED (2026-06-30, observed): esbuild 0.21.5 bundles src/mcp-server.ts into a SINGLE 1.1MB ESM file (vfkb-mcp.mjs, --bundle --platform=node --format=esm) with @modelcontextprotocol/sdk + zod inlined and ZERO externals. Run from a throwaway dir with NO node_modules (VFKB_DIR=tmp brain), it completed the MCP initialize handshake and tools/list advertised all 9 kb_* tools. So RFC-010 Open Item #1 is closed: the 'truly zero-dep single-file MCP server' is feasible — FR-2 does NOT need the vendored-bundle-dir fallback. Only the size warning (1.1MB) — cosmetic. cli.ts can bundle the same way for vfkb.mjs.

ACCEPTED RFC-010 -> ADR-0030: the consumer integration & distribution contract. vfkb becomes a consumable product for non-self repos via 5 FRs built in dependency-forced order: FR-2 portable engine ($VFKB_HOME indirection + single-file esbuild bundles vfkb.mjs/vfkb-mcp.mjs, kills the relative dist/ paths) -> FR-1 vfkb init (idempotent scaffold) -> FR-4 brain<->engine version stamp (.vfkb/manifest.json, committed) + vfkb doctor -> FR-3 vfkb import (mykb/adr/markdown, lossy) -> CONSUMER-ONBOARDING.md. The FR-2 single-file-bundle unknown was spiked & resolved BEFORE acceptance (fact 8c547ae0). DoD = scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs, an agent-driven sandbox scenario extending the ADR-0028 wiring smoke-gate self->consumer, must-fail contrast arm; live self-repo wiring migrates to $VFKB_HOME only AFTER the consumer sandbox proves it. Roadmap re-ratified with Track 7 (active frontier). Three defaults locked in the ADR: manifest.json committed (vs derived index-meta), lossy mykb import, $VFKB_HOME as a documented one-time per-machine step.

esbuild bundle (FR-2): do NOT add a `banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' }` when bundling src/cli.ts / src/mcp-server.ts. Both entry files already start with a shebang, which esbuild preserves at line 1; the banner emits a SECOND shebang on line 2 -> 'SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token' when node loads the .mjs. Fix: no banner — let esbuild hoist the source shebang. scripts/build-bundles.mjs builds dist/bundles/{vfkb.mjs (~48KB, zero-dep), vfkb-mcp.mjs (~1.1MB, @modelcontextprotocol/sdk+zod inlined)}; src/bundles.test.ts is the deterministic inner gate (runs both from a no-node_modules cwd: MCP tools/list=9, CLI add persists).

FR-1 `vfkb init [project]` shipped (src/init.ts + cli.ts dispatch + src/init.test.ts, 5 tests). Idempotently scaffolds a consumer repo: .vfkb/entries.jsonl (never clobbers an existing brain), .mcp.json (merges, keeps other servers), .claude/settings.json (3 hooks: SessionStart/PreToolUse-gate/Stop, merged per-event), .gitignore stanza (append-once), AGENTS.md snippet (append-once, marker <!-- vfkb:how-we-track-work -->); prints the one manual step (set $VFKB_HOME + approve MCP/hooks). WIRING-FORM CONTRACT (consumers depend on this): .mcp.json args use ${VFKB_HOME}/vfkb-mcp.mjs (Claude Code env-var EXPANSION in args), while .claude/settings.json hook commands use shell `node \"$VFKB_HOME/vfkb.mjs\" hook <sub>` (shell expansion). project name defaults to the cwd basename. 111/111 unit green.

Consumer-onboarding capability DEMONSTRATED 3/3 (2026-06-30, ADR-0030/0029 DoD). scenarios/consumer-onboarding.mjs drives REAL claude turns: PASS arm = a fresh repo onboarded via `vfkb init onboard-demo` (portable $VFKB_HOME bundles, NO node_modules in the sandbox) + one recorded fact -> a real claude turn answers the seeded sentinel ZEBRA-7731 (the SessionStart auto-layer delivered the repo's vfkb knowledge). CONTRAST/must-fail arm = a NOT-onboarded repo -> same turn replies UNKNOWN (trial 3: agent explored the empty dir, found nothing). 3/3 both arms. Exercises FR-1 (init) + FR-2 ($VFKB_HOME single-file bundle) end-to-end with a real agent. A deterministic pre-check (SessionStart injection must surface the seed) fails cheap before any model call. SessionEnd hook contract EMPIRICALLY VERIFIED (CLI v2.1.196) + cross-checked vs official docs (code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks.md). FINDINGS: (1) SessionEnd DOES fire (observed on `claude -p` completion); per docs it also fires on /clear, logout, resume, crash, Ctrl-C — it CANNOT block/delay exit (fire-and-forget, exit code IGNORED). (2) It CAN run arbitrary shell commands (10-min timeout per docs); cwd = the project dir (observed PWD=sandbox), so `git add .vfkb && git commit` targets the right repo — GAP-2 auto-commit is FEASIBLE. (3) stdin JSON fields: session_id, transcript_path, cwd, prompt_id, hook_event_name, reason. NOTE: NO last_assistant_message (unlike the Stop hook) — to derive a handoff the hook must READ transcript_path (.jsonl). (4) `reason` enum (docs): clear|resume|logout|prompt_input_exit|bypass_permissions_disabled|other. DISCREPANCY: docs say `-p` => prompt_input_exit, but OBSERVED `claude -p "arg"` => reason:"other" (prompt_input_exit is for PIPED stdin EOF, not an arg prompt). So a reason-matcher is brittle — prefer running on ALL reasons (or guard inside the hook). (5) stdout IS schema-validated: emitting {hookSpecificOutput:{additionalContext}} (the Stop-hook shape) FAILS validation with a visible (non-fatal, exit 0) warning. additionalContext does NOTHING on SessionEnd (session is ending — CANNOT inject into next session). Allowed output fields: continue, suppressOutput, stopReason, decision(approve|block), reason, systemMessage, terminalSequence, permissionDecision. => the impl should emit NOTHING (silent run = no warning, confirmed). DESIGN IMPLICATION for Track 8: SessionEnd can AUTO-COMMIT the brain (GAP 2) but CANNOT prompt the agent for a handoff (GAP 1) — the prompting must stay on the Stop hook (which CAN inject); SessionEnd only commits / optionally auto-derives a handoff from the transcript. Re-verify if CLI version != 2.1.196.

GAP-1 design constraint (verified 2026-06-30): KB_SESSION_ID is NOT set in this repo's live hook wiring (.claude/settings.json hookCommand is just `VFKB_DATA_DIR=.vfkb VFKB_PROJECT=vfkb node ...bootstrap... cli hook <sub>` — no KB_SESSION_ID; bootstrap doesn't set it either). SessionState.load() returns an EPHEMERAL in-memory state when KB_SESSION_ID is unset (file=null) -> `vfkb resume-note` does NOT persist, and NO per-session record is written during normal hook operation. The two records in .vfkb/.sessions/ (a UUID + a hand-named graph-session) were created when an id WAS set / by hand. IMPLICATIONS: (1) the cleanest B2 GAP-1 variant — "promote the session resume-note to a committed handoff entry" — is NON-VIABLE as wired (no persisted note to promote). (2) Any session-record-based GAP-1 mechanism is unreliable here. (3) The ONLY robust state-free signal at hook time is the git-HEAD-delta of entries.jsonl (append-only, ADR-0019) — exactly what stop-reminder.ts already uses (uncommittedDecisionCount/hasUncommittedWork, no KB_SESSION_ID). So a viable GAP-1 must be git-delta-based: B1 (Stop nudge: fire when uncommitted work AND no uncommitted handoff/next fact since HEAD) or B2 (SessionEnd: if no handoff/next entry among new-since-HEAD, write a minimal auto handoff enumerating the session's new entries). (4) SEPARATE QUESTION worth confirming: whether the SessionStart resume digest (ADR-0020) is actually exercising session records here at all, given KB_SESSION_ID is unset — the digest may be running off an empty/ephemeral session + the brain only.

RFC-012 (Track 9 Q1, proposed): deterministic contradiction surfacing at write time. On every explicit kb_add/add, run a deterministic lexical conflict check (findConflictCandidates: live entries sharing type-or-tag with distinct-stemmed-term overlap over threshold, extending the ADR-0021 findLexicalDuplicates seam) and append candidate conflicts to the ADD RESULT — the write always lands first (D3d unchanged), never blocks, never auto-supersedes, no LLM on the write path (TOKI). Passive capture exempt. Plus a propose-only 'curate conflicts' sweep. DoD: L4 contradiction-surface (seeded stale value + corrected info in-task; vfkb arm ends with one live truth vs contrast arm with contradictory duplicates; RED first, both harnesses) + unit fixtures for the detector.

Why: arXiv:2606.27472 shows agents do not supersede stale memories unprompted and more budget does not help; vfkb lived it — gotcha 91338268 stayed live-and-wrong for 2 days after its fix landed, caught only by an unrelated inventory. Contradictions are near-misses, so the exact-match curate-time dup check can never catch them; write time is when the fresh info is in-context.

vfkb v2 vision doc (proposed/pre-RFC consolidation, not decided): docs/V2-VISION.md. Consolidates the 2026-07-05 brainstorm into initiatives: concurrency/merge safety (git merge driver for entries.jsonl tail-append conflicts, advisory lock for the TOCTOU gap, per-entry session_id/agent_id attribution), schema honesty (real `why` field, full envelope validation at the read boundary, formalized contradiction/supersede fields), scale (rebuildable index instead of readAll()'s full-file rescan per call), a pluggable storage-backend interface (JSONL stays the ADR-0019 default; hosted is opt-in, not a reversal), and known warts (GAP-1, per-scenario L4 record digests, a concurrent-writer test in the pyramid, RFC-003 revisit reasoning). Central correction+insight in §1: the "reuse mykb's multi-project mechanism for multi-session" framing was subtly wrong — verified against IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md's Lessons-from-mykb record and session.ts: L4 (one global `.active` pointer -> concurrent sessions silently clobber) was ALREADY specifically the multi-session fix (KB_SESSION_ID-keyed `.sessions/<id>.json`, day one), a separate axis from mykb's single-brain-for-many-projects concern (settled differently via per-project brain, D2e/D2g; VFKB_PROJECT is confirmed just a doctor-consistency label, not a scope key). The mechanism already has the right shape (partition-by-id, never a shared mutable singleton) and already exposes SessionState.records() as a de facto concurrent-session registry — it just isn't engaged: verified none of this repo's 4 live hook commands in .claude/settings.json set KB_SESSION_ID, so SessionState.load() is always ephemeral today (matches the existing GAP-1 finding). Concrete cheap fix specified: Claude Code hook stdin already carries a real session_id per invocation (verified earlier for Stop/SessionEnd payloads) -> cli hook <sub> should read it from stdin instead of depending on an env var nothing sets. That closes GAP-1 outright and is the foundation several other v2 initiatives build on.

V2-VISION.md's §1 fix (read session_id from Claude Code hook stdin) was stress-tested against a second, very different real harness — vilonotes' kagent-hosted researcher — by reading ~/VFKB/vilonotes/researcher/run.py and backend/app.py directly (not assumed). RESULT: the fix generalizes cleanly (vilonotes' own .claude/settings.json wires the same 4 hooks, none set KB_SESSION_ID either, so GAP-1 independently reproduces there and the stdin-based fix needs zero changes to run.py). But the deep-dive surfaced a NEW, more serious, previously-unrecorded gap distinct from anything else in the doc: in-pod `kb_add` writes are NOT durably reconciled. The researcher pod calls kb_add live against its own local .vfkb/entries.jsonl (mcp__vfkb is in its ALLOWED_TOOLS), but the durable write path (backend/app.py's _land_commit) runs in a totally separate throwaway git worktree off origin/main and SYNTHESIZES ITS OWN single fact string ("SESSION HANDOFF..."/"ARTIFACT COMMITTED...") — it never reads or merges the pod's actual local entries.jsonl content. Compounding this, refresh_clone()'s own docstring admits prior-session kb_add writes are exactly what the NEXT new session's `git reset --hard origin/<branch>` would destroy (refresh is throttled to once-per-new-session specifically to bound, not eliminate, this loss window). Net effect: anything the researcher records live via kb_add beyond the one curated fact the backend later synthesizes is scratch state — survives at most until the next session starts on that pod, never reaches main. Also found: vilonotes already has an uncommitted `_turn_lock = asyncio.Lock()` (on branch feat/parallel-research-sessions) serializing ALL turn execution across ALL sessions in the pod — a coarse, hand-rolled, whole-turn-granularity workaround built at the orchestration layer because vfkb itself offers no finer-grained lock primitive; this is the concrete argument for vfkb owning its own lock scoped to just the read-decide-append critical section. Full writeup in docs/V2-VISION.md §2.

vfkb v2 development uses a dedicated long-lived `v2` branch; `main` stays release-only and never receives v2 code. Every v2 initiative from docs/V2-VISION.md branches from `v2` and PRs back into `v2`, never into `main`. `main` keeps receiving v1 hotfixes as before; `main` is merged into `v2` regularly (after every hotfix, not batched) to avoid divergence. Exception: docs (RFCs/ADRs/vision notes, incl. V2-VISION.md itself) are non-breaking and keep landing on `main` via the normal PR flow. `v2` gets the same branch protection as `main` (no direct pushes, PR required). Cutover (merge v2 into main, or promote v2 to be the new main) is a future decision, not this one. Full record: ADR-0036. Why: v2 is explicitly breaking-changes-allowed (the roadmap re-ratification CLAUDE.md's "Current state" already flagged as the next fork), but main is the released version consumers (incl. vilonotes via kagent) depend on staying stable — developing v2 directly on main would force freezing v1 fixes or leaking half-built breaking changes into the release. This is the standard pattern for a breaking next-major developed alongside a still-supported release.
